lines.text: Tucker here. Stationed near the Vilas Park perimeter, south edge of Lake Mendota. The clock reads 02:00. We're {{ELAPSED}} in and the numbers are moving in ways the modeling didn't predict.

Madison-wide cumulative is sitting at 139 kills as of 01:52. That's 17 above the 2168 pace at this exact hour — we were at 122 last year. The cold front is suppressing activity in the northern zones, but the East Side is compensating. Hard.

Atwood and Schenk's Corners are running at 24% of total volume with only 8 hours elapsed. Last year at this mark they were 19%. Weapons profile is also shifting — bladed work is up 11 percentage points. Cheaper. People are getting creative because they have to.
I'm looking at the Mendota shoreline right now and there are — hold on, my equipment is — the relay is getting interference from something. Two boats out on the water. Neither is official.

Holden, the State Street corridor methodical sweep from 23:00 — if that pattern holds and replicates, we could be looking at sustained 20+ kills per hour for the remaining 4 hours. That would put us in the 240-250 range by dawn. That's territory we haven't been in since—

tickerItems.text: Madison cumulative: 139 kills (02:00 mark). YoY +17 vs 2168 same hour.
East Side (Atwood/Schenk's) at 24% of night total. +5 percentage points vs 2168 pace.
Bladed weapon incidents up 11pp YoY. Firearms ammunition costs cited as economic driver.
Mendota shoreline: two civilian vessels active in darkness. No official response in service area.
